Ilirska Bist

(pronounced [[mɔˈziːɾjɛ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Slovene "Help:IPA/Slovene"); [Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Občina Mozirje*) is a [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") in the traditional region of [Styria](/wiki/Lower_Styria "Lower Styria") in northeastern [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"). The seat of the municipality is the town of [Mozirje](/wiki/Mozirje "Mozirje"). Mozirje became a municipality in 1994.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

[*Vzpostavitev lokalne samouprave v Republiki Sloveniji v številkah*](https://www.stat.si/StatWeb/File/DocSysFile/1290). Ljubljana: Statistični urad Republike Slovenije. 2007. p. 86. Retrieved February 22, 2020.

The **Municipality of Mozirje** (pronounced [[mɔˈziːɾjɛ]](/wiki/Help:IPA/Slovene "Help:IPA/Slovene"); [Slovene](/wiki/Slovene_language "Slovene language"): *Občina Mozirje*) is a [municipality](/wiki/Municipality "Municipality") in the traditional region of [Styria](/wiki/Lower_Styria "Lower Styria") in northeastern [Slovenia](/wiki/Slovenia "Slovenia"). The seat of the municipality is the town of [Mozirje](/wiki/Mozirje "Mozirje"). Mozirje became a municipality in 1994.[[2]](#cite_note-2)

## Settlements

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Municipality_of_Mozirje&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Settlements")]

In addition to the municipal seat of Mozirje, the municipality also includes the following settlements:

* [Brezje](/wiki/Brezje,_Mozirje "Brezje, Mozirje")
* [Dobrovlje pri Mozirju](/wiki/Dobrovlje_pri_Mozirju "Dobrovlje pri Mozirju")
* [Lepa Njiva](/wiki/Lepa_Njiva "Lepa Njiva")
* [Ljubija](/wiki/Ljubija,_Mozirje "Ljubija, Mozirje")
* [Loke pri Mozirju](/wiki/Loke_pri_Mozirju "Loke pri Mozirju")
* [Radegunda](/wiki/Radegunda,_Mozirje "Radegunda, Mozirje")
